title: Rango Tel
content:

{{Quote|For every bounty hunter that can do the job, there are a thousand more who think they can.|Crime lord saying|<ref name="db1" />}}
'''Rango Tel''' was a particularly dim-witted [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who hailed from the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Sernpidal/Legends|Sernpidal]]. Growing up as a street ruffian, an instance of utter luck led him to believe that it was his life's calling to become a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]]. Defeating a drunkard in an unruly bar brawl, Tel ended up collecting a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] he never pursued and winning the allegiance of the [[Pa'lowick/Legends|Pa'lowick]] [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Aneesa Dym]]. With Dym at his side, Tel set out into [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] in the [[Starship/Legends|starship]] ''[[Dusty Duck]]'' and was quickly swindled into wearing an inappropriate vac-exposure suit that he believed was [[Mandalorian armor/Legends|Mandalorian armor]], consequently earning the derisive nickname '''"Tank Head."'''

Calling upon a contact from his youth, the racketeer [[Orin Nyell]], Tel was given his first bounty: the [[Fluggrian/Legends|Fluggrian]] [[Kam Nale]], whose family threatened Nyell's smuggling operations on [[Ploo IV/Legends|Ploo IV]]. Eager to make a name for himself in the bounty hunting trade, Tel set off with Dym to track down Nale, eventually locating him on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. The Fluggrian had involved himself with the local [[Podracing/Legends|podracing]] circuit in [[Mos Espa/Legends|Mos Espa]], leading Tel to corner him in an alley shortly before the [[Boonta Eve Classic (32 BBY)/Legends|Boonta Eve Classic]]. Having placed too much faith in the protective abilities of his vac-exposure suit, Tel was gunned down by Nale, bringing an abrupt halt to the short-lived Sernpidal native's career.

==Biography==
===Early life===
[[Reproduction/Legends|Born]] and raised on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Sernpidal/Legends|Sernpidal]], the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] Rango Tel lived his young life as a disenfranchised street ruffian in the planet's slums.<ref name="db1" /><ref name="mag7">{{SW|url=news/the-not-so-magnificent-seven|text=The Not-So Magnificent Seven|int=The Not-So Magnificent Seven|nolive=1}}</ref> During his youth, Tel was involved with the local racketeer [[Orin Nyell]], and did a few odd jobs for him.<ref name="mag7" /> After several [[Standard year/Legends|years]], as a young man, he was drawn into a [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] brawl where he inadvertently incapacitated a [[Neeamesh Dym's murderer|patron]] with a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] on their head for the [[Death/Legends|murder]] of [[Pa'lowick/Legends|Pa'lowick]] [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Neeamesh Dym]].<ref name="db1" /><ref name="mag7" /> Though Tel had initially been unaware of the bounty, he ultimately claimed it and began to believe that it was his destiny to enter the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] profession.<ref name="db1" /> The client was Neeamesh Dym's daughter, [[Aneesa Dym|Aneesa]], but unfortunately for Tel she had already spent all of her savings pursuing the killer across the galaxy. As an alternative to payment, Aneesa Dym offered Tel both her father's [[Light freighter/Legends|freighter]], the ''[[Dusty Duck]]'', and her services as the ship's pilot as he embarked on his new career as a ruthless bounty hunter. Tel accepted, eager to pursue his career away from Sernpidal<ref name="mag7" />—as the partnership commenced, Dym became smitten with her new Human companion.<ref name="db2">{{DB|starship|dustyduck|Dusty Duck}}</ref>

===Bounty hunter===
When it came to inspiration for Tel's new image as a bounty hunter to be feared in the criminal underworld, he drew from the iconography of the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]], specifically the [[Basilisk war droid/Legends|Basilisk war droid]]-riding warriors who had served [[Mandalore the Indomitable]] against the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], rather than the pacifist [[New Mandalorians/Legends|New Mandalorians]] who had recently ascended to power on [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]] following the [[Great Clan Wars/Legends|Great Clan Wars]]. Hoping to secure his own set of [[Mandalorian armor/Legends|Mandalorian armor]], Tel traveled to [[Feriae Junction/Legends|Feriae Junction]],<ref name="mag7" /> and came across a [[Unidentified junk dealer|junk dealer]] of ill repute and sought to purchase equipment for his new endeavor. Based on the direct request for Mandalorian armor, the [[Junk dealer/Legends|junk dealer]] doubted Tel's intelligence. Passing off a vac-exposure suit as the genuine article, the dealer gleaned a handsome sum from Tel, and Tel, due to his ungainly appearance, earned the nickname "Tank Head."<ref name="db1" /> He did not realize that the nickname was meant to be insulting.<ref name="cswe">{{CSWECite|III|228|Tel, Rango "Tank Head"}}</ref>

Eager to begin work, Tel called upon his old contact Orin Nyell and hoped to gain patronage in his bounty hunting career. Nyell, who was either sympathetic to Tel or humoring him for his own amusement, gave "Tank Head" a mark: [[Kam Nale]], a [[Fluggrian/Legends|Fluggrian]] whose father, [[Borzu Nale|Borzu]], had competed with Nyell on the [[spinward]] world of [[Ploo IV/Legends|Ploo IV]], specifically around the racketeer's [[Water/Legends|water]]-smuggling operation. Borzu had recently been assassinated by the [[Glymphid/Legends|Glymphid]] [[Aldar Beedo/Legends|Aldar Beedo]], and Nyell feared that Kam would take over his late father's business.<ref name="mag7" /> Tel, eager to impress, accepted the job.<ref name="db1" />

===The hunt for Kam Nale===
[[File:RangoTel-TPMHD.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Rango Tel strides into Mos Espa in search of Kam Nale.]]
With Aneesa Dym in tow,<ref name="db2" /> Tel began tracking Nale. As Nale had no desire to follow in his father's footsteps and enter into illegality, he used a series of aliases to elude capture and death, evading Tel for [[Standard month/Legends|months]]. Although Tel eventually learned that Nale had no ill intent toward his employer, he persisted, locating Nale—or "Elan Mak," the pseudonym that Nale was operating under—on the Outer Rim world of [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]. "Mak" was to participate in the [[Boonta Eve Classic (32 BBY)/Legends|Boonta Eve Classic]] [[Podracing/Legends|podrace]], and it was there that Tel hoped to corner and kill Nale.<ref name="db1" />

Leaving Dym behind to oversee repairs to the ''Dusty Duck'',<ref name="db2" /> Tel set off into the [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Mos Espa/Legends|Mos Espa]] in search of his mark.<ref name="db1" /> During his search, Tel wandered into the [[Poodoo Lounge]] and attended the [[Pre-Boonta Concert]] featuring podracer [[Boles Roor/Legends|Boles Roor]] in the hopes that "Elan Mak" would show. After hunting for several days, Tel arrived at the Boonta Eve Classic early, in the hopes of finally achieving something for the first time in his life.<ref name="mag7" /> Shortly before the race, he spoke with<ref name="tpm">{{Film|I}}</ref> bounty hunter [[Rum Sleg/Legends|Rum Sleg]]<ref name="Sleg">{{YJCCG|set=Boonta Eve Podrace|cardname=Rum Sleg|link=boontaevepodrace/dark/large/44rumslegbountyhunter.html}}</ref> in the streets of Mos Espa.<ref name="tpm" />

Tel cornered Nale in a back alley, hoping to kill the elusive Fluggrian and claim his second bounty.<ref name="db1" /> Tel placed his faith in his faux-Mandalorian armor, believing that it would protect him against any retaliation in the inevitable quick-draw duel between him and Kame Nale.<ref name="mag7" /> However, Nale was too fast, his heavy [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]] making short work of Tel and his substandard equipment. Tel was killed before his career had even begun, and Nale walked away unscathed, continuing to [[Podracer (profession)/Legends|podrace]] under the "Elan Mak" alias. Dym never learned of Tel's fate,<ref name="db1" /> as she was murdered shortly after by the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]] in an unrelated incident.<ref name="db2" /> Tel's attempted hit on Nale did have its repercussions, however–the Fluggrian, more cautious after the attack, returned to Ploo IV and took up his father's mantle as Nyell had feared.<ref name="mag7" />

==Personality and traits==
Rango Tel stood 1.7 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall and had [[Color/Legends|fair]] skin. He grew up in a harsh environment, living as a street ruffian on Sernpidal who was prone to violence. Tel originally had no desire to become a bounty hunter, and it was sheer happenstance that caused him to decide upon his career. He also lacked prior knowledge going into the bounty hunter trade, and was of the belief that [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] armor was easy to come by. Tel's ignorance led him to pay a copious amount of [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] for a suit that was clearly not of Mandalorian make. Not only clueless, he was additionally overconfident, in that he thought himself capable of murdering Kam Nale. That overconfidence, however, proved to be his fatal weakness. Particularly dim-witted, Tel did not realize that the nickname "Tank Head" was insulting,<ref name="db1" /> and could not pick up when the racketeer Orin Nyell was toying with him.<ref name="mag7" />

Although he lacked the requisite skill to succeed as a bounty hunter, Tel did enjoy a streak of luck, not only being able to call on a contact from his youth in Nyell to give rise to his prospective bounty hunting career, but also in chancing upon his first quarry in a drunken brawl, without even realizing what he was doing. Tel also understood, to a degree, the importance of image in the bounty hunting trade, drawing upon the imposing and intimidating elements of the defunct Mandalorian warrior culture, without having the proper understanding of how to execute such an image.<ref name="mag7" />

===Relationship with Aneesa Dym===
During his brief bounty hunting career, Tel worked alongside the pilot Aneesa Dym. When her father was fatally stabbed by a dissatisfied customer, Aneesa Dym relentlessly hunted the murderer, although she also placed a bounty on the villain's head. The bounty was claimed by Tel, and as a result, Dym fell in love with the Sernpidal citizen. Together they pursued Tel's one and only bounty, Kam Nale, in the ''Dusty Duck'', with Dym providing her services as a pilot of her late father's [[Starship/Legends|ship]]. Their relationship persisted until 32 BBY, when both perished on the same day in unrelated killings.<ref name="db2" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Other than that, my submission is almost entirely intact. My only regret is that I told so much of Tel's story; there's not much chance of him cropping up somewhere in the EU. But at the same time, his story couldn't really be told if he had survived it; part of his character was that he had to die on this first bounty hunting mission. So I guess I'll just keep looking for a crime lord to mumble something about a thousand people who think they can do a bounty hunter's job.|Aaron Sinner|<ref name="blog" />}}
Rango Tel first appeared in [[George Lucas]]'s [[1999]] film [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']]<ref name="tpm">{{Film|I}}</ref> as an unnamed background extra seen briefly during the film's introduction to the city of Mos Espa. He was played by an uncredited actor. Tel was later one of the first characters to have his backstory elaborated on through the [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]] "[[What's The Story?]]" feature on the website [[StarWars.com]]. An entry on Tel in the site's official [[Databank (original)|Databank]] was written by [[Aaron Sinner]],<ref name="db1" /> who originally named the character "Ip Numeth." The organizers of the competition instead chose "Rango Tel," a name contributed by the contestant "[[Captain Daultay Dofine]]," and additionally created the nickname "Tank Head."<ref name="blog">{{Blog|kiaaron|2|The Gambler and the Bounty Hunter}}</ref>

Sinner chose Sernpidal as Tel's [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] due to having located it on a map of the ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] and found it "sufficiently backwater." After watching Tel's lone scene in ''The Phantom Menace'', Sinner was reminded of Mandalorian armor, and he used the armor as the basis of the entire article. He felt that the story which he crafted necessitated the dim-witted character's death. ''Star Wars'' author [[Abel G. Peña]] has named Tel's Databank entry as the most hilarious and endearing of any "What's The Story?" character.<ref name="blog" />

The extra can be seen in the audience at the Boonta Eve Classic later in the film.<ref name="tpm" /> As Rango Tel's "What's The Story?" entry would establish he was killed before that event,<ref name="db1" /> [[in-universe]], it is a different character portrayed by the extra in that later scene.
